title: Plugin Management
description: Loading, registering, activating, and deactivating Composer plugins

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: External developer who creates a composer-plugin package implementing PluginInterface

actors.name: Composer
actors.description: The main Composer application that owns the PluginManager and drives lifecycle calls

use_cases.name: Load Installed Plugins
use_cases.description: Scans local and global repositories for composer-plugin / composer-installer packages and registers each one
steps: Sort packages with PackageSorter (plugin-modifies-install-path weighted first)
For each CompletePackage of type composer-plugin or composer-installer: call registerPackage()

use_cases.name: Register Plugin Package
use_cases.description: Validates API version, checks allow-plugins config, autoloads classes, instantiates and activates plugin
steps: Check isPluginAllowed() → prompt user or throw PluginBlockedException
Skip if already in registeredPlugins map
For composer-installer type: instantiate as InstallerInterface, add to InstallationManager
For composer-plugin type: instantiate PluginInterface class → call addPlugin()

use_cases.name: Activate Plugin
use_cases.description: Activates a plugin instance and subscribes it to events
steps: Guard: isPluginAllowed() → skip with debug message

use_cases.name: Deactivate Plugin Package
use_cases.description: Deactivates all plugin instances registered under a package
steps: For each plugin: if InstallerInterface → InstallationManager::removeInstaller(); else → removePlugin()

use_cases.name: Deactivate Plugin Instance
use_cases.description: Removes a single plugin instance from the active list

use_cases.name: Uninstall Plugin
use_cases.description: Deactivates and then notifies plugin of uninstall
steps: For each plugin: removePlugin() then uninstallPlugin()

use_cases.name: Check Plugin Allowed
use_cases.description: Checks allow-plugins config rules; prompts user interactively if not configured; throws PluginBlockedException if denied
steps: Match package name against allowPluginRules / allowGlobalPluginRules patterns
If rules null (legacy lock): throw RuntimeException in non-interactive mode
If interactive and no rule: prompt user y/n/d → persist answer to composer.json
If optional and not allowed: silently return false

use_cases.name: Query Plugin Capabilities
use_cases.description: Collects capability implementations from all active plugins that implement Capable
steps: Instantiate capability class; validate it implements Capability and requested interface

use_cases.name: Implement Plugin
use_cases.description: Developer implements PluginInterface (activate / deactivate / uninstall) and optionally EventSubscriberInterface or Capable
steps: Define composer.json: type=composer-plugin, require composer-plugin-api ^2.9, extra.class=<FQN>

use_cases.name: Disable Plugins
use_cases.description: Prevents all (or local/global) plugins from loading
steps: arePluginsDisabled(type) returns true → all load/add calls are silently skipped

key_relationships: PluginManager uses InstallationManager (addInstaller / removeInstaller for legacy installer plugins)
PluginManager uses PackageSorter (dependency-aware sort before load/deactivate)
guards_and_constraints: Package must require composer-plugin-api with matching version constraint
extra.class must be defined and implement PluginInterface
allow-plugins config must explicitly permit the package (prompts interactively or throws PluginBlockedException)
symfony/flex < 1.9.8 is blocked for Composer 2 compatibility
Duplicate registration is silently skipped (registeredPlugins map guard)
